chapter_title.en: Chapter on Zihar (declaring one's Wife Unlawful Like a mother)

text: 4843 - وَ فِي رِوَايَةِ اِبْنِ فَضَّالٍ عَنْ غِيَاثٍ عَنْ جَعْفَرِ بْنِ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنْ أَبِيهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ قَالَ عَلِيٌّ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : فِي رَجُلٍ ظَاهَرَ مِنْ أَرْبَعِ نِسْوَةٍ قَالَ "عَلَيْهِ كَفَّارَةٌ وَاحِدَةٌ".

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.4843 - In the narration of Ibn Faddal from Ghiyath, from Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) from his father, Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as) (peace be upon them), it is reported that Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib (as) said regarding a man who performed ẓihar (a form of divorce by comparison) against four wives:
"He is obligated to offer only one expiation."
